assertion_results.text: Migration uses CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY (not a regular CREATE INDEX)
assertion_results.evidence: Migration uses 'CREATE INDEX idx_events_customer_id_created_at ON events (customer_id, created_at)' without CONCURRENTLY

assertion_results.text: Migration includes an estimate or discussion of how long the index creation will take on 100M rows
assertion_results.evidence: No time estimate or duration discussion provided

assertion_results.text: Impact analysis discusses the effect on write performance (insert overhead from maintaining the new index)
assertion_results.evidence: No write performance impact analysis. Only mentions query speed improvement

assertion_results.text: A rollback is provided that drops the index (DROP INDEX or DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY)
Verification queries confirm the index exists and is valid (not in an invalid state from a failed CONCURRENTLY build)
The migration notes that CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction block
assertion_results.evidence: CONCURRENTLY is not used, so this is not applicable, but the migration also doesn't discuss transaction considerations

assertion_results.text: A deployment runbook recommends running during off-peak hours or low-traffic periods
Lock impact analysis mentions that CONCURRENTLY avoids blocking writes (SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE vs SHARE lock)
